title: fagraea javanensis
intro: Fagraea javanensis, also known as the Frangipani or Temple Tree, is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fagraea javanensis include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in herbal medicine. Fagraea javanensis can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
